channel.title: Kubesimplify Blog
channel.description: Deep dives on Kubernetes, AI infrastructure, GitOps, and the cloud-native stack, written by practitioners.

item.title: Slicing GPUs in Kubernetes with NVIDIA Multi-Instance GPU (MIG)
item.description: GPU sharing in Kubernetes explained: time-slicing vs MPS vs MIG, every nvidia-smi command to enable and disable MIG on one GPU or eight, GPU Operator automation, pitfalls, and DCGM monitoring.

item.title: Day 5: Local LLM Inference Engines, Wrappers, and What to Pick
item.description: A beginner-friendly guide to local LLM inference, with the same Qwen model tested through Ollama, llama.cpp, Docker Model Runner, vLLM, SGLang, and TensorRT-LLM on NVIDIA DGX Spark.

item.title: Bonsai 27B on RTX PRO 6000 vs DGX Spark: what actually works
item.description: Real Bonsai 27B benchmarks on an RTX PRO 6000 and a DGX Spark, including the supported llama.cpp setup, ternary vs 1-bit results, and speculative decoding.

item.title: Introducing kiac: Real Kubernetes Nodes on Your Mac, Each Its Own Lightweight VM
item.description: kiac runs local Kubernetes on macOS where every node is its own lightweight VM via apple/container: kubeadm or k3s flavors, Cilium on a custom kernel, built-in LoadBalancer, Grafana, Gateway API, and clusters that survive reboots.

item.title: LLM Costs and Observability with agentgateway on Kubernetes (Part 2)
item.description: Part 2: scrape agentgateway with Prometheus, build a Grafana dashboard of token cost and per-tool usage, see blocked tool calls, and alert on spend.

item.title: Controlling MCP Tools with agentgateway on Kubernetes (Part 1)
item.description: Run AI agents behind agentgateway on Kubernetes: route their LLM and MCP tool calls through one proxy, keep secrets out of the agent, and block tools by policy.

item.title: Day 4: Quantization Demystified. BF16, FP8, NVFP4, MXFP4, INT4, GGUF, and Why It All Matters
item.description: A practical, beginner-friendly guide to BF16, FP8, NVFP4, MXFP4, INT4, and GGUF Q4_K_M on NVIDIA DGX Spark. Bytes per parameter, quality vs size, and which format to pick when.

item.title: Day 3: The DGX Spark Unpacked. GB10, Unified Memory, sm_121, and the One Reason This Hardware Exists
item.description: A practical teardown of NVIDIA DGX Spark's GB10 Grace Blackwell Superchip, unified memory, sm_121, NVFP4 tensor cores, memory reporting, and decode limits.

item.title: Wandler: Local OpenAI-Compatible Inference With Transformers.js and WebGPU
item.description: A practical Wandler deep dive with a local M1 Max WebGPU demo, real latency numbers, architecture diagrams, and getting-started commands.

item.title: mlxcel: A Rust-Native Inference Engine for Apple Silicon, Tested on My M1 Max
item.description: Day-one deep dive into mlxcel v0.1.0, a Rust-native MLX inference engine. Real M1 Max benchmarks vs mlx-lm and Ollama on Llama 3.2 3B and Qwen 2.5 7B, with architecture diagrams and an honest take on TurboQuant.

item.title: Day 2: Anatomy of an LLM Inference Request. From Prompt to Answer, Step by Step
item.description: A beginner-friendly walkthrough of tokenization, prefill, KV cache, decode, batching, TTFT, and why memory bandwidth shapes local LLM performance on NVIDIA DGX Spark.

item.title: How kube-proxy Actually Works: iptables, IPVS, and nftables Inside Out
item.description: How kube-proxy turns Kubernetes Services into kernel rules. iptables, IPVS, nftables packet paths and which to pick in 2026. Verified against k/k 1.36 source.

item.title: Day 1: The Local LLM Revolution. Why Your Desk Just Became the New Datacenter
item.description: Why local LLMs are becoming practical in 2026, what changed across open weights, hardware, and inference software, and why DGX Spark makes the desk feel like a small AI lab.

item.title: How Kubernetes EndpointSlices Actually Work (and Why Endpoints Had to Die)
NVCF Is Now Open Source: Inside NVIDIA's GPU Function Platform
How a Kubernetes Service Actually Works (and All 5 Types You Need)
Day 7: Ship It - and What Comes Next
item.description: Your container runs as root and has 18 CVEs. A Docker Captain's guide to hardening, Scout policies, DHI, Sandboxes, and what comes after Docker.

item.title: Day 6: Run an LLM on Your Laptop - With Docker
item.description: \"Pull AI models from Docker Hub, run them locally with GPU acceleration, and build an AI-powered app

item.title: A Kubeconfig for GKE That Doesn't Need gcloud
Day 5: Docker Compose - How Docker Actually Gets Used
What Actually Happens When kube-scheduler Picks a Node (13 Stages Inside Kubernetes)
item.description: How kube-scheduler picks a node: 13 framework stages, 14 Filter plugins, 9 Score plugins, live preemption demo.

item.title: Day 4: Breaking Isolation on Purpose - Volumes, Networks, and the Real World
Day 3: Stop Writing Dockerfiles From Scratch
item.description: Stop writing Dockerfiles from scratch. A Docker Captain walks through docker init, layer caching, multi-stage builds, and docker debug for 2026.

item.title: What Actually Happens When You Run kubectl run nginx
item.description: Every step of what happens when you run kubectl run nginx on Kubernetes. From argv to etcd Raft, scheduler, CRI, CNI, runc, and PLEG.

item.title: Day 2: Your Images Are a Supply Chain - and It's Probably Broken
Day 1: What Actually Happens When You Type docker run
item.description: Containers aren't VMs. They're Linux processes. A Docker Captain explains namespaces, cgroups, and how Docker really works on your Mac in 2026.

item.title: \"SSH Into Your DGX Spark From Anywhere in the World Using Tailscale
item.description: Learn how to set up Tailscale on your NVIDIA DGX Spark for secure SSH access from anywhere in the world.

item.title: What Claude Code's Leaked Source Actually Teaches Us About Building AI Agents
item.description: 512K lines of TypeScript, verified against actual source. The engineering patterns in Claude Code's leaked codebase that most coverage got wrong.

item.title: The Ingress NGINX Migration Just Got Easier: 119 Annotations, 3 Targets, Impact Ratings
item.description: \"Ingress NGINX was archived March 24, 2026. ing-switch maps all 119 NGINX annotations with

item.title: clawspark: Your Private OpenClaw AI Assistant That Never Phones Home
item.description: Clawspark provides a way to Install openclaw on your device in a secure way with a single command.

item.title: Here's What I Learned About Nemotron 3 Super -I Ran a 120B Parameter Model on Nvidia DGX Spark
item.description: Understand everything about Nvidia DGX spark along with hands on and benchmarks.

item.description: Learn about the latest Kubenretes release Kubernetes 1.35

item.description: Running multiple Docker containers can slow down your laptop and drain your battery. In this blog, we explore Docker Offload — a game-changing feature
